title: fix(env): parsing --env incorrect in cli
body: Fixed: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19096#issuecomment-1670164724 Fixed: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19096#issuecomment-1670613061 The root cause of this issue lies in the fact that the shell discards the quotes in the parameters, as shown in the following code snippet: ```shell podman run -e K='a#b' alpine printenv aaa # To podman run -e K=a#b alpine printenv aaa ``` This will result in problems with the current `parseEnv` implementation. Unfortunately, there is no way to change the behavior of the shell, so the only option is to fix it within `parseEnv`. In order to reduce code complexity, I have ported the previous `parseEnv` function back and renamed it as `parseEnvWithSlice`. Furthermore, this function will be used exclusively for the `--env` option, which will bring another benefit: it will run faster than `--env-file`. Now: ```shell $ podman run --env aaa='aaa\nbbb#ccc\nddd' alpine printenv aaa aaa\nbbb#ccc\nddd ``` #### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change? ```release-note Fix the issue where the parsing of the `--env` parameter value is incorrect. ``` ---- Ref: 1. https://github.com/spf13/cobra/issues/898 2. https://stackoverflow.com/questions/53896081/how-to-keep-quotes-in-os-args 3. https://github.com/golang/go/issues/30952 PTAL @rhatdan @vrothberg @Luap99 /cc @edsantiago @kajinamit

@BlackHole1: GitHub didn't allow me to request PR reviews from the following users: kajinamit.

Note that only [containers members](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) and repo collaborators can review this PR, and authors cannot review their own PRs.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19560):

>Fixed: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19096#issuecomment-1670164724
>Fixed: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19096#issuecomment-1670613061
>
>The root cause of this issue lies in the fact that the shell discards the quotes in the parameters, as shown in the following code snippet:
>
>```shell
>podman run -e K='a#b' alpine printenv aaa
># To
>podman run -e K=a#b alpine printenv aaa
>```
>
>This will result in problems with the current `parseEnv` implementation. Unfortunately, there is no way to change the behavior of the shell, so the only option is to fix it within `parseEnv`. In order to reduce code complexity, I have ported the previous `parseEnv` function back and renamed it as `parseEnvWithSlice`. Furthermore, this function will be used exclusively for the --env option, which will bring another benefit: it will run faster than `--env-file`.
>
>#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?
>
>```release-note
>Fix the issue where the parsing of the `--env` parameter value is incorrect.
>```
>
>----
>
>Ref:
>1. https://github.com/spf13/cobra/issues/898
>2. https://stackoverflow.com/questions/53896081/how-to-keep-quotes-in-os-args
>3. https://github.com/golang/go/issues/30952
>
>PTAL @rhatdan @vrothberg @Luap99 
>/cc @edsantiago @kajinamit 


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md). @vrothberg Thank you for your CR. I modified done.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *BlackHole1*, *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). > /lgtm /hold @BlackHole1 PTAL

@rhatdan Are you pinging the wrong person? 🤣

Yes. I meant to ping the reporter of the bug.
/hold cancel
I'm going to backport this to the 4.6 branch.
I don't think the original `env-file` PR made it into 4.6
I'm upset that this got merged without my review. There are still problems with env-file processing. I will open up new issues.

Just noting for future reference, that if this fix is backported into Podman 4.4, it has the potential to break OpenStack on that version of RHEL (9.2).  Please do NOT backport this to the 4.4 branch without very careful consideration.  Please note: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2230212 for details.

What breaks OpenStack(actually it does not break the whole OpenStack but only TripleO which is the deployment tool) is not this one but https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/19096 , thought as long as that is backported along with this follow-up PR then it would not cause problem with the usage in RHEL9.2 .
